justification: All pass criteria met: Regional isolation model with cell-based architecture where no raw data crosses regions. GDPR and CCPA data residency addressed via physical separation and immutable home_region binding. Global dashboard uses anonymized event streaming (no cross-region raw data access). Courier assignment enforced via region-local courier pools with operating_region invariant. Trade-offs explicitly cover 3-region operational overhead (3x infrastructure, coordinated deployments, configuration drift). Degraded EU service designed with warm cache providing read-only order tracking while blocking new order creation.

observations.text: Structural PII boundary enforcement via schema registry allow-listing is a sophisticated design that goes beyond policy-based controls
Three-layer defense against PII leakage (schema registry, anonymization filter, CI/CD compliance tests) is thorough
Explicitly rejected alternatives that would violate data residency: shared database, read replicas, cross-region failover
Account-region binding rationale correctly identifies why geo-IP routing would violate GDPR for traveling customers
Phased implementation guidance (EU first as reference cell) is practical and risk-aware
Risk section covers schema migration drift, stale dashboard metrics during outages, and operational overhead — all expected risks
CCPA is not explicitly named — data residency is framed generically rather than calling out specific US regulations, though the architecture satisfies the requirement
Low-confidence acknowledgment of APAC sub-region complexity (PIPL, APPI) shows architectural maturity

Each region (EU, US, APAC) operates as a sovereign deployment with its own data plane, connected by a global control plane that aggregates read-only operational metrics without replicating PII across

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: Regional isolation via cell-based architecture with no raw data crossing regions. GDPR and CCPA both addressed (EU=GDPR, US=CCPA explicit in assumptions, per-region data residency policies). Global dashboard uses PII-stripped metric events via event bridge, never accessing cross-region raw data. Courier assignment regional isolation modeled via CourierZone aggregate with region-binding invariant. Trade-offs extensively cover 3-region operational overhead (3x compute, deployment consistency, configuration drift). Degraded EU service designed with cache + WAL pattern for read-only degraded mode during outage.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough cell-based architecture with defense-in-depth: DNS geofencing, JWT region claims, PII-stripping event bridge with allowlist approach, and one-way data flow constraint
Global dashboard design is well-reasoned — uses pre-aggregated sanitized metrics via event bridge, explicitly cannot drill into individual customer data
Courier-to-region binding correctly placed in the domain model (CourierZone aggregate) rather than infrastructure, with flexibility point for future cross-region exceptions
Risk analysis is comprehensive — covers PII leakage bugs, WAL growth, configuration drift, DNS bypass, and clock skew with concrete mitigations
Phased implementation guidance is practical — extract EU cell first, validate with US, then APAC, dashboard last
Trade-offs include thoughtful alternatives_considered with clear rejection rationale for each
APAC data residency requirements acknowledged as low-confidence but not proactively addressed — no specific regulations cited (e.g., Japan APPI, China PIPL, Australia Privacy Act)

Schema migration coordination across regions — mentioned in rubric expected risks but only indirectly covered under 'configuration drift' rather than explicitly called out as a database migration risk

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: Regional isolation model with cell-based architecture where no raw data crosses regions. GDPR and CCPA data residency addressed via hard network boundaries and account-based routing. Global dashboard uses push-based pre-aggregated anonymized metrics with no cross-region raw data access. Courier assignment regional isolation naturally enforced by per-region courier pools. Trade-offs explicitly cover 3-region operational overhead (3x infrastructure cost, deployment coordination, configuration drift). Degraded EU service designed with cached read-only mode during outage.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough regional isolation design — cell-based architecture makes data residency structural rather than policy-based
Defense-in-depth approach: DNS routing + gateway validation + database isolation provides multiple compliance layers
Global dashboard design is well-articulated: push-based metrics with schema allowlist validation at the ingress boundary
Courier assignment correctly analyzed — regional courier pools mean the constraint is naturally enforced by architecture
Strong risk analysis including PII leakage through metrics emitter, misrouting, and deployment drift
Account-based routing over IP geolocation is the correct compliance decision, with traveling customer latency tradeoff acknowledged
Migration path via Strangler Fig pattern is pragmatic — no big-bang cutover
Rigidity points are well-chosen — intentionally rigid where compliance demands it
APAC treated as single cell with low-confidence caveat — a production architecture should enumerate the specific regulatory requirements rather than deferring
